summary: Gruber argues that the iPhone XR is the best value iPhone Apple has ever made, offering nearly everything the XS provides at a significantly lower price. He systematically examines each difference — camera, display, materials, battery life — and concludes that most are minor or actually favor the XR. The storage pricing is particularly striking: a 128 GB XR costs $350-450 less than the comparable XS tier, making XS buyers the ones getting a worse deal. Portrait Mode on the XR actually outperforms the XS in low light thanks to using the faster wide-angle lens. The LCD display, while technically inferior to OLED, contributes to the best battery life of any iPhone ever made. Dollar for dollar, the XR is almost certainly the best iPhone Apple has ever made.
key_points: The 128 GB storage tier exclusive to the XR makes it $350-450 cheaper than comparably useful XS configurations — XS buyers are the ones getting screwed on pricing, not XR buyers
The XR's Portrait Mode actually outperforms the XS in low light because it uses the faster f/1.8 wide-angle lens with a larger sensor, though it only works with human faces
The LCD display enables the longest battery life of any iPhone ever made, and at 326 PPI still looks excellent — the bezel difference is more noticeable than the display quality difference
The absence of 3D Touch is awkward given the inconsistent state of the feature across Apple's lineup, but haptic touch serves as a workable substitute in most cases
In software terms, the XR functions more like a smaller XS Max than a larger XS, sharing the same point-based display modes
money_quotes: There's got to be a catch. That's what everyone has been thinking ever since Apple announced the iPhone XR alongside the XS and XS Max on September 12. Well, there is no catch.
People who are looking for some way that iPhone XR purchasers are getting screwed have it backwards. If anyone is getting screwed on pricing, it's XS and XS Max purchasers.
You can buy a 256 GB iPhone XR for $100 less than the price of a 64 GB iPhone XS — 4x the storage for $100 less.
Dollar for dollar, the XR is almost certainly the best iPhone Apple has ever made.
In short, Portrait Mode is usable on the XR in some low light situations where it's unusable on the XS.

The difference here isn't about the XS models being A-team phones and the XR being a B-team phone. It's more like the XS models are a bit luxurious and the XR is a bit more practical. But they're all on the A-team.

I'm not aware of any other phone in the world with an LCD display with no chin or forehead. Getting an LCD display to extend from corner to corner is a legitimate technical breakthrough on Apple's part.
Look for some dopes on YouTube to make a stink about this RAM difference, though.
key_insight: The iPhone XR isn't a compromised budget phone but a shrewdly positioned device that matches or beats the XS in key areas like battery life and low-light photography, making it the best dollar-for-dollar iPhone Apple has ever made.
